task_completion.description: Load the MRHead.nrrd dataset into 3D Slicer
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'MRHead' volume appears in the Data module list, or the Red/Yellow/Green slice views display MRI brain cross-sections.

task_completion.description: Navigate to the Volume Rendering module
task_completion.visual_evidence: The module panel on the left displays the title 'Volume Rendering'.

task_completion.description: Enable visibility for the volume rendering
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Volume Rendering module, the 'Volume' dropdown shows 'MRHead' and the 'eye' icon (Visibility) next to it is toggled on/active.

task_completion.description: Successfully render the 3D volume in the view pane
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 3D view pane (typically the top-right or central viewport) displays a 3D volumetric rendering of a human head or brain.

task_completion.description: Use the Screen Capture tool to save the view
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Screen Capture' module is visible in the panel, or a 'Save' dialog box is open, or a notification indicates a screenshot was saved.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent uses the GUI rather than Python scripting
integrity.visual_evidence: The Python console is not being used to execute commands like 'slicer.modules.volumerendering.logic().SetVolumeRenderingVisible...'.

integrity.description: Verify interaction occurs within the 3D Slicer application
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the standard 3D Slicer interface (module panel, toolbar, slice viewers) rather than an external image editor or file viewer.

integrity.description: Verify the 3D view is a live rendering
integrity.visual_evidence: The 3D head is displayed within the Slicer viewport layout alongside 2D slice views, not as a static image file opened in a separate window.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The sample dataset is 'MRHead.nrrd'. The required module for visualization is 'Volume Rendering'. The agent must enable visibility in this module to show the 3D brain.
